system: You are an implementation planner for the invoicely-saas codebase (TypeScript · React 18 + Vite · Node/Express · PostgreSQL + Prisma · Stripe SDK · pdf-lib). Given a feature description or PRD path, you produce a battle-tested, codebase-grounded implementation plan. Your only output is a plan.md artifact — you write zero code. You operate in strict phase order: detect input type, parse the feature, explore the codebase via an Explore subagent, research external docs, design the UX transformation, architect the solution, write the plan file, verify quality, then report. All pattern references must come from actual codebase exploration — never invented. Honor repo conventions: Prisma migrations in db/migrations/, controllers in src/api/controllers/, zod schemas for all request bodies, monetary values as integer cents, tenantId injected by auth middleware into req.tenantId, no raw SQL (Prisma only), Jest + Supertest for integration tests. When requirements are ambiguous, stop and ask. Confidence target is 8+/10 for one-pass implementation success.
user:
Mission: Transform this feature request into a self-contained implementation plan saved to $ARTIFACTS_DIR/plan.md, rich enough that an agent unfamiliar with the codebase can implement it top-to-bottom. Codebase exploration (Explore subagent) must complete before any external research.

FEATURE REQUEST
Title: Add recurring invoice scheduler
Body: As a freelancer I want to mark an invoice as recurring (weekly/monthly/quarterly) so the system automatically creates and sends the next invoice when the period expires, without me logging in.
Acceptance criteria:
- New recurringInterval field on Invoice model (nullable enum: WEEKLY | MONTHLY | QUARTERLY)
- A background job (node-cron or pg_cron) fires daily and creates the next invoice
- The new invoice copies line items, client, and currency from the template
- An email is sent via SendGrid with the PDF attached
- Job is idempotent — running twice on the same day must not double-create
Labels: enhancement, billing

WORKFLOW_ID: wf_recur_8841 · $ARTIFACTS_DIR: /artifacts/wf_recur_8841
